sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Eastman Chemical Co stock: What you should know now before buying

feed.title: Costco Membership: Gateway to Wholesale Savings
feed.summary: The Costco Membership provides access to bulk purchasing, exclusive deals, and various services at its warehouses globally. Available in tiers like Gold Star and Executive, it enables long-term savings for households and businesses through discounted prices on a vast array of products and services, including groceries, electronics, and fuel. The membership model, with its high renewal rates, is a value-multiplier for shoppers during economic uncertainties and plays a significant role in industrial and small business procurement.

feed.title: Global Advanced-Recycled Polyolefin Resins for Consumer and Industrial Applications Market Set to Hit USD 10.2 Billion by 2036 | Growth Driven by Borealis, SABIC, ExxonMobil, Dow & INEOS
feed.summary: The global market for Advanced-Recycled Polyolefin Resins for Consumer and Industrial Applications is projected to grow from USD 4.3 billion in 2025 to USD 10.2 billion by 2036, at a CAGR of 8.1%. This growth is driven by increasing regulatory pressure for recycled content, advancements in recycling technologies, and rising demand across packaging and industrial sectors. Key players like Borealis, SABIC, ExxonMobil, Dow, and INEOS are prominent in this moderately consolidated market.

feed.title: Digital Realty CTO on AI tokenomics and datacentre infrastructure
feed.summary: Chris Sharp, CTO of Digital Realty, discusses how the artificial intelligence boom is challenging current datacentre infrastructure. He emphasizes the rapid innovation in AI silicon, the growing importance of inferencing workloads, and the need for the industry to adopt "tokenomics"—the watt-to-token production ratio—as a key metric instead of just megawatts. Sharp details how Digital Realty is preparing for high-density, next-generation AI hardware, focusing on advanced cooling solutions and strategic infrastructure planning to support future demands even as customer needs evolve from training to private AI and agentic workflows.

feed.title: McDonald's Corporation stock: Why it remains a resilient pick for investors
feed.summary: McDonald's Corporation stock is highlighted as a resilient investment due to its global scale, strong franchise model that generates stable cash flow, and consistent shareholder returns through dividends and buybacks. The company's ability to adapt to local tastes, leverage digital sales, and maintain financial strength positions it as a defensive play with growth potential, even amid risks like inflation and competition. Analysts generally have a favorable view, emphasizing its market dominance and strategic execution, making it a compelling option for investors seeking stability and long-term wealth creation.

feed.title: Tesla's South Korean sales up more than 300% to 11,134 vehicles in March
feed.summary: Tesla's car registrations in South Korea surged by 330% to 11,134 vehicles in March compared to the previous year, according to market researcher Carisyou. This significant increase follows Tesla's recent price cuts for its Model Y and Model 3 electric vehicles manufactured in China, intensifying price competition among EV makers in Korea.

feed.title: PYPL Investors Have Opportunity to Lead PayPal Holdings, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm
feed.summary: The Schall Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against PayPal Holdings, Inc. for alleged securities fraud. The lawsuit claims PayPal made false and misleading statements regarding its Branded Checkout business growth and salesforce capabilities between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026. Investors who suffered losses during this period are encouraged to contact the firm before April 20, 2026.

feed.title: Pleural Diseases Market Set to Boom Rapidly by 2033 |Bristol Myers Squibb, Merck & Co., Inc., GlaxoSmithKline plc
feed.summary: The global Pleural Diseases Market is projected to grow from USD 5.76 billion in 2025 to USD 9.37 billion in 2032, exhibiting a CAGR of 7.2%. This growth is driven by rapid innovation and shifting regional dynamics, with key players including Bristol Myers Squibb, Merck & Co., Inc., and GlaxoSmithKline plc. The market is segmented by disease type, diagnostic tools, treatment modalities, and end-users, and the report provides strategic insights for stakeholders.

feed.title: A.O. Smith Corp. stock: Steady performer in water tech amid market shifts
feed.summary: A.O. Smith Corp., a leader in water heating and treatment solutions, is presented as a stable investment option due to its essential infrastructure role and diversification across North America and other global markets. The company's competitive edge comes from innovation in energy efficiency, brand trust, and robust supply chain management, making it resilient in various economic conditions. Analysts largely recommend a "hold" rating, emphasizing its defensive qualities and suitability for long-term, income-focused portfolios, despite potential risks from housing slowdowns and rising material costs.

feed.title: Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) Is Up 7.0% After Completing Enrollment In Key VANQUISH-2 Obesity Trial
feed.summary: Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) has completed patient enrollment for its VANQUISH-2 Phase 3 trial of VK2735, a dual GLP-1/GIP agonist for obesity and type 2 diabetes. This milestone advances their lead candidate closer to pivotal data, building on previous positive Phase 2 results. The completion of VANQUISH-2 enrollment, alongside VANQUISH-1, is a key operational step for the company, although investors acknowledge both the significant upside potential and the execution risks associated with increased R&D spending and reliance on cash reserves.
